title: Presentation / HTML slide deck
description: Data contract for the slide-deck format: a full-viewport 16:9 HTML PPT with scroll-snap navigation. Each slide occupies one full screen (one idea per slide); the deck renders as a vertical scroll-snap container with a fixed progress bar, slide counter, and keyboard nav. Renders to a self-contained, themeable HTML file (default theme: Purple). The sample's #report-data is a valid instance of this schema.

meta.description: Shared report chrome (org / project / title / theme). Brand-neutral: keep {{ORG}} / {{PROJECT}} / {{AUTHOR}} placeholders unless real values are supplied.

reportType.description: Eyebrow label shown above the deck title, e.g. 'Executive Readout' or {{REPORT_TYPE}}.

title.description: Deck title used in the browser <title> and cover slide, e.g. 'FY26 Q2 Executive Readout'.

subtitle.description: Optional one-line standfirst shown on the cover slide below the main title.

date.description: ISO date the deck was produced (YYYY-MM-DD).

author.description: Author or owning team, shown in the cover byline or {{AUTHOR}} placeholder.

theme.description: Initial brand theme; applied as <html data-theme>. The in-deck theme picker can change it live. Default for this format is 'purple'.

slides.description: The ordered list of slides in the deck. Each slide occupies one full 16:9 viewport. Render order matches presentation order. Typical deck: 8–16 slides.

id.description: Stable unique slide identifier used for deep-linking (e.g. '#3'). Typically a 1-based integer or a short slug.

type.description: Slide layout variant. Determines which content fields are relevant and which CSS class (.slide-cover, .slide-agenda, etc.) is applied.

eyebrow.description: Optional small tracked-mono label shown above the title on most slide types (e.g. 'Context', 'Section 02', 'Key finding').

title.description: Main slide headline (30–48px on screen). Required for most types; omit only on purely visual slides.

subtitle.description: Optional supporting line shown under the title (.deck-sub). Appropriate for cover, section, and bullets slides.

chips.description: Optional small status/info pills (.chip / .st-badge) shown on cover and close slides (e.g. verdict status, date chip).

byline.description: Optional key/value pairs shown in the byline row on the cover slide (e.g. [{key:'Presented by', value:'{{AUTHOR}}'}, {key:'Date', value:'2026-06-22'}]).

bullets.description: For type='bullets': the bullet list items (3–6 items is ideal; each item is ≥18px on screen). Also used for the close slide's next-steps list when items have an 'owner' field.

text.description: Bullet text. May contain basic inline emphasis (bold). Keep to one sentence.

tone.description: Optional semantic tone applied to the bullet icon box: 'ok', 'risk', 'hold', or 'brand' (default).

icon.description: Optional house icon key from the icons/ catalog (e.g. 'check', 'alert', 'arrow-right').

owner.description: For close/next-steps bullets: the responsible owner or team.

due.description: For close/next-steps bullets: the due date or deadline (ISO date or natural language, e.g. '2026-06-30').

agendaItems.description: For type='agenda': the ordered list of sections to be covered. Renders as a numbered list.

index.description: Display index shown in the large mono column (e.g. 1, '01').

note.description: Optional short annotation or time estimate shown in the right column (e.g. '10 min', 'slides 4–7').

current.description: If true, this item is highlighted as the current position in the deck (is-here state).

sectionNumber.description: For type='section': the large two-digit section number displayed prominently (e.g. '01', 2).

sectionProgress.description: For type='section': optional list of section identifiers (same length as total sections) used to render the progress pip row at the bottom of the divider slide.

cols.description: For type='two-col': exactly two panel definitions (left and right). Each panel has a heading and a body expressed as either a key/value list or a bullet list.

heading.description: Panel heading shown in mono small-caps.

accent.description: If true, the panel gets a top accent border (.panel.accent).

kvRows.description: Optional key/value rows rendered in this panel.

items.description: Optional bullet/prose items rendered in this panel.

stats.description: For type='stat': one to three KPI cells shown as oversized tnum numbers. Each cell renders as a .kpi block with a large figure, a label, and an optional note and trend.

unit.description: Optional unit appended to the value in smaller type, e.g. '%', 'ms', 'tps'.

label.description: Short descriptive label in mono small-caps below the number, e.g. 'Phase 2 investment'.

note.description: Optional one-line context shown under the label.

trend.description: Optional trend indicator text, e.g. '+12% YoY', '↑ vs Q1'.

trendDir.description: Direction of the trend for colour: 'up' (ok/green) or 'down' (risk/red).

tone.description: Override tone applied to the KPI number colour: 'brand' (default), 'ok', 'risk', 'hold'.

chart.description: For type='chart': a single CSS/SVG bar or line chart. Axis labels, series legend, and a one-line takeaway heading.

series.description: One or more data series. For a bar chart each series is a set of category/value pairs. For a line chart each series is an ordered list of values at the same x-positions.

name.description: Series name shown in the legend.

values.description: Data values. Align positionally with the shared categories array.

categories.description: X-axis category labels (shared across all series).

maxValue.description: Optional explicit max for the value axis (for consistent scale across slides). Defaults to the data maximum.

tableData.description: For type='table': a single data table (≤6 rows recommended so it reads from a projector). Renders using the .deck-table style.

numeric.description: If true, the column is right-aligned with tnum font.

rows.description: Data rows. Each row is an array of cell values aligned to headers.

tone.description: Optional mini-badge tone for status cells: 'ok', 'hold', 'risk'.

quote.description: For type='quote': a single large pull-quote with optional attribution. Renders with lots of negative space.

text.description: The pull-quote text (28–40px). One sentence or a very short paragraph.

compare.description: For type='compare': an A-vs-B two-panel comparison layout. Each side has a heading, a short label, and a list of positive/negative points.

vsLabel.description: Label in the centre divider element (default 'VS').

optionA.description: The left-hand comparison panel (typically the status quo or less-favoured option).

heading.description: Panel heading in mono small-caps (e.g. 'Option A · Current state').

label.description: Short option name shown in large type (e.g. 'Full vendor renewal').

positive.description: true = ok/green check; false = risk/red alert.

optionB.description: The right-hand comparison panel (typically the recommended option).

label.description: Short option name shown in large type.

recommended.description: If true, this panel gets the win highlight (.cmp-pane.win).

footnote.description: Optional small-print footnote line rendered at the bottom of the slide stage (mono, faint). Useful on close slides for source references.

slides.title: GEN2 Core Banking — Steering Committee
slides.subtitle: Programme health, financials, and vendor decision for committee approval

slides.title: Programme health & KPIs
slides.subtitle: Where we stand at end of FY26 Q2

bullets.text: Integration test pass rate reached 94.2% — above the 90% gate.
UAT cutover window moved to 2026-09-14 (+3 weeks) due to vendor API delay.
Compliance sign-off outstanding; CISO exception request filed.
Team capacity stable; no attrition risk flagged this quarter.

slides.title: Monthly spend is tracking ¥60M above plan
Two paths to renewal — one clear winner

cols.heading: Option A · Full SaaS renewal
items: Locks in vendor roadmap for 5 years.

slides.title: Option B is the recommended path

items.text: Fastest time to first release (5 months).

quote.text: "The hybrid path gives us optionality we can actually execute. The cost case alone makes it defensible to the board."

slides.title: Three open risks require a decision today
What we ask of the committee today

bullets.text: Approve the hybrid vendor renewal (Option B).
Accept or direct a scope re-cut on the compliance exception.

slides.footnote: {{PROJECT}} · Executive Readout · FY26 Q2 — full evidence in the long-form QBR
